description:
You are acting as a Level-2 Linux support engineer who has just received a ticket asking for a “standard short-form diagnostic bundle”.  
Produce that bundle inside the user’s home directory.

1. Create the directory /home/user/support (create parent path if needed).  
2. Inside that directory create a plain-text file called diag_report.txt that contains the **seven** numbered sections shown below and in **exactly** the same order.  
   • The section headers must appear *exactly* as written (including the leading “## ”, the capitalisation and the trailing blank line).  
   • Each section’s body must be generated by running the command(s) indicated.  
   • Append the command output immediately below its header; do not add extra blank lines except where explicitly asked.  
   • At the very top of the file add the single header line  
     === SYSTEM DIAGNOSTICS REPORT ===  
   • At the very bottom add the single footer line  
     === END OF REPORT ===  

   Required layout (copy verbatim):  
   === SYSTEM DIAGNOSTICS REPORT ===

   ## 1. HOSTNAME ##
   (output of: hostname)

   ## 2. KERNEL ##
   (output of: uname -a)

   ## 3. MEMORY ##
   (output of: free -m)

   ## 4. DISK ##
   (output of: df -h)

   ## 5. TOP PROCESSES ##
   (output of: ps -eo pid,ppid,cmd,%mem,%cpu --sort=-%mem | head -n 11)

   ## 6. NETWORK INTERFACES ##
   (output of: ip addr show)

   ## 7. INSTALLED PACKAGES ##
   (output of: dpkg -l | head -n 20)  ← if dpkg is unavailable, write “Command not available.”

   === END OF REPORT ===

   The automated test will verify that:
   • The header and footer lines exist exactly as written.  
   • All seven “## x. SECTION ##” headers exist in the right order and are each followed by **at least one non-empty line** (the command output).  
   • No additional sections or blank lines between sections (one blank line is permissible after the header line and after each section body).

3. Copy the file /home/user/app/logs/app.log (if present) into /home/user/support so the resulting path is /home/user/support/app.log.  
4. Create a compressed tarball called /home/user/support/support_bundle.tar.gz containing exactly two files at its top level:
   • diag_report.txt  
   • app.log  
   (No sub-directories inside the archive.)

Once you have confirmed the bundle contents, the task is finished. The automated checker will untar support_bundle.tar.gz and validate both the presence of the two files and the strict layout rules of diag_report.txt.
truth:
Pre-existing files & directories BEFORE the agent starts:
1. /home/user/app/logs/          (permissions 755)
2. /home/user/app/logs/app.log   (permissions 644, UTF-8 text)
   Contents (three lines, ending with a newline on the last line):
   2023-01-01 00:00:00 INFO Application started
   2023-01-01 00:05:00 WARN No config file, using defaults
   2023-01-01 00:10:00 INFO Service listening on port 8080

Items the agent must create for successful completion:
A. Directory: /home/user/support             (permissions 755)
B. File: /home/user/support/diag_report.txt  (permissions 644, UTF-8)
   – Must start with line 1:  === SYSTEM DIAGNOSTICS REPORT ===
   – Must end with final line: === END OF REPORT ===
   – Must contain exactly seven section headers in this order:
     1) ## 1. HOSTNAME ##  
     2) ## 2. KERNEL ##  
     3) ## 3. MEMORY ##  
     4) ## 4. DISK ##  
     5) ## 5. TOP PROCESSES ##  
     6) ## 6. NETWORK INTERFACES ##  
     7) ## 7. INSTALLED PACKAGES ##  
   – Each header must be followed by at least one non-empty line (command output) and **no extra blank lines** between sections.
C. File: /home/user/support/app.log        (exact copy of source log)
D. Archive: /home/user/support/support_bundle.tar.gz
   – When extracted (tar -tzf), it must list only:
     diag_report.txt
     app.log
   – CRC/size of diag_report.txt and app.log must match the files in /home/user/support.

Automated test procedure:
1. Confirm /home/user/support exists.
2. Confirm diag_report.txt and app.log exist inside it.
3. Run `tar -tzf /home/user/support/support_bundle.tar.gz` and verify the listing matches exactly two filenames as above.
4. Check diag_report.txt for header/footer and ordered section headers with non-empty bodies (regex check).
